title: angelica czernaevia var. flaccida
intro: Angelica czernaevia var. flaccida, also known as the angelica or dong quai, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It is a member of the carrot family and is often used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including being an antispasmodic and a diuretic. The plant's roots, stems, and leaves are used to make herbal preparations that are believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing menstrual cramps and improving circulation, as well as acting as an anti-inflammatory agent to soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Angelica czernaevia var. flaccida is often dried or made into a tea to be consumed for its medicinal properties.
